output.final_deliverable:
I cannot complete the requested verified path because the merged browsing results are empty (no visit/search evidence was provided). To meet your mandate’s critical extraction requirements I must fetch and record the actual pages and the links between them. Below I give a proposed path (with full URLs), the rationale for each step, and exactly what evidence is missing. If you want, I will now visit each page in order and produce the verified path with citations showing which page links to which.

Proposed path (four pages, in order):
1. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence
   - Why this page: This is the required start page you specified.
   - What I will check on this page: confirm a link to the next page (likely “Machine learning”).
2. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning
   - Why this page: Machine learning is a central subfield of AI and the AI page commonly links to it; it commonly contains links to methods and subareas including quantum variants.
   - What I will check on this page: confirm a link to “Quantum machine learning” (or directly to “Quantum computing” if present).
3. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_machine_learning
   - Why this page: Quantum machine learning is the intersection of ML and quantum computing; that page should link to the broader “Quantum computing” article.
   - What I will check on this page: confirm a link to “Quantum computing.”
4. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_computing
   - Why this page: This is your required target page.

What’s missing (required to satisfy the mandate and CRITICAL extraction requirements):
- I do not currently have page content or visit logs for any of the above pages. Specifically missing are:
  - The actual HTML or textual content of https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Artificial_intelligence to show which link I clicked (anchor text and target URL).
  - The actual HTML or textual content of https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning to show the link to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_machine_learning (or directly to Quantum computing).
  - The actual HTML or textual content of https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_machine_learning to show the link to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quantum_computing.
  - Evidence that each page contains the link to the next page (for adjacency verification). The mandate requires explicitly showing which URL links to which (e.g., “URL[i] contains link to URL[i+1]”).

Next step I can take (please confirm):
- I will visit the start page (Artificial intelligence), record the exact link I follow (anchor text and target URL), then visit each subsequent page and record the same, producing a step-by-step verified path with direct URL citations and explicit statements of linkage (e.g., “Artificial intelligence page contains a link to Machine learning at [exact anchor] -> https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Machine_learning”).

If you confirm, I will perform those visits and return the fully verified path, with numbered steps, the exact anchor text used, and explicit adjacency statements showing which page links to which, per your requirements.

reasons: The agent did not perform any page visits or provide the required visit/search evidence (HTML/text or visit logs) showing that each page links to the next; it explicitly stated the browsing results were empty.
The task required reaching and documenting a verified path to the 'Quantum computing' page; the agent only provided a proposed (unverified) path and offered to perform the visits if confirmed.

The proposed path (Artificial intelligence → Machine learning → Quantum machine learning → Quantum computing) is plausible and the link-choice rationales are logical, which merits partial credit for planning.

However, critical requirements were not met: there is no explicit proof that each page contains the link to the next (no citations, anchor text, or evidence of adjacency), so the core objective was not completed.

details.reasoning_quality: The agent's reasoning about which intermediate pages to follow is sensible and clearly explained. It correctly identifies likely intermediary topics (Machine learning, Quantum machine learning) and explains how they connect to Quantum computing. The main shortcoming is lack of verification: the agent did not fetch or record the actual pages and links as required, but it clearly described what evidence is missing and proposed concrete next steps to obtain it.
